name: karta-framework-bench
purpose: A periodic, deterministic-first benchmark of the karta plugin itself (not of any consumer project): measure execution-flow enforcement gaps, SME pack appropriateness and fillable gaps, dark-area stabilization, per-phase performance, output quality, doc/repo parity, and field adoption — version-over-version, with committed machine-diffable results. Doctrine: enforced checks and frozen denominators over skippable prose; every metric must be honest under growth (append-only ledgers, per-cohort attribution, epoch joins for LLM arms). Assembled 2026-07-17 against karta 2.21.0 at /mnt/agent-storage/vader/src/karta, with consumer field repos /mnt/agent-storage/vader/src/parchmark and /mnt/agent-storage/vader/src/gringotts. Seeding artifacts from the audit session live at /tmp/claude-1000/-mnt-agent-storage-vader-src-karta/0fe6f672-22b7-4c17-8abe-f8084f939961/scratchpad/: karta-map.json (subsystem audit map) and evaltool.json (buy-vs-build tool verdicts). BLOCKING FIRST STEP of any build-out: commit copies into the repo as benchmarks/flow/seed-map-2026-07-17.json and benchmarks/research/evaltool-2026-07-17.json — the scratchpad is session-scoped and will expire.

chassis.one_liner: Pinned promptfoo as suite chassis wrapping headless `claude -p` (exec provider, isolated CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR) for executable arms; standalone stdlib python probe scripts composed by one blocking gate runner for the deterministic lane; owned miner.py/reduce.py/trend.py fill the per-phase-telemetry and git-native-trend gaps no surveyed tool provides.
chassis.path_convention: All bench assets live under the pre-existing benchmarks/ tree of the karta repo (benchmarks/flow/, benchmarks/perf/, benchmarks/field/, benchmarks/sme/, benchmarks/fixtures/, benchmarks/probes/, benchmarks/results/, benchmarks/gate/, benchmarks/bench/ for the chassis toolchain). Any 'bench/' spelling in earlier drafts is normalized to 'benchmarks/'. Shared fixtures get one registry: benchmarks/fixtures/REGISTRY.json with sha-pinned manifests (see open_questions).
chassis.chassis_decision: promptfoo (MIT, pinned via benchmarks/bench/package.json + `npx promptfoo@<pin>`) is THE chassis — one chassis for both vector families. Every scored run, executable or audit, is a promptfoo suite: providers produce outputs, assertions score them, --output writes the per-run JSON that reduce.py folds into the committed result file. The system under test is invoked through promptfoo's exec/custom-script provider wrapping a literal `claude -p` shell script — NOT the claude-agent-sdk provider — because karta is a plugin whose value is skills + six hook guards firing under real settings, and only a real headless Claude Code process with a bench-controlled CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR exercises that; it also rides the owner's subscription auth (no API key for the SUT) and needs no Docker, which neutralizes the Incus indirection for the default path. Audit vectors run under the same chassis: deterministic probes are an exec provider (`python benchmarks/probes/run_probes.py --target <karta-worktree>`, zero model calls) whose JSON findings promptfoo asserts on; qualitative audits are llm-rubric/agent-rubric assertions over karta's own files. Runners rejected as chassis: Harbor (Docker-sandbox-first fights Incus-only Docker; demoted to task-format donor), Inspect AI (same sandbox problem, heavier than a one-person periodic bench), crucible (1-star anonymous alpha, dead since June — isolation pattern copied, code not adopted), skill-creator evals (subagent-scoped; machinery cribbed as components). promptfoo's two known gaps — per-phase telemetry and git-native longitudinal comparison — are filled by two small owned components (miner.py, trend.py). RECONCILIATION with the sharpened vectors (which dropped promptfoo/bayes_evals per-vector because neither is installed today and n<=3 makes intervals theater): the deterministic lane never depends on promptfoo — every deterministic vector is a standalone python/bash script runnable directly and composed by benchmarks/gate/run_gate.py; promptfoo is adopted only when the Tier-1/Tier-2 executable arms are built, as the orchestrator over those same scripts; vendored bayes_evals is used only for paired comparisons at k>=5 (Tier 2), and every n<=3 arm reports raw counts. This reconciliation is recorded as a buy-vs-build row in ownership-cost-ledger; see open_questions[0].

components.role: Chassis: suite/arm orchestration, deterministic asserts (python/javascript/regex/json-schema/trajectory), llm-rubric + agent-rubric judge lanes (agent-rubric gets read-only workspace access to inspect produced diffs), per-run --output JSON
components.why: Research verdict BUY: only candidate natively covering claude-as-SUT, the A/B skill-pack pattern, both scoring modes, and plain committed files with zero standing services

components.role: SUT substrate behind the exec provider: builds an isolated CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR with the karta version under test installed, clones the fixture template into scratch with a FRESH git init per trial, runs claude -p --output-format json, emits {output, metadata:{session_id, cost_usd, num_turns, wall_clock}}
components.why: The only way real plugin skills and all six hook guards actually fire; subscription auth; CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR pinning is the version-over-version axis

components.role: Donor of the task.yaml field set: instruction + fixture + verifier + oracle-must-pass + timeouts + canary string
components.why: De-facto 2025-26 standard for terminal-agent tasks; oracle-must-pass self-validation is the discipline the hand-rolled sme bench lacked

components.role: Per-task grading_mode {automated | llm_judge | hybrid}; 'rerun the same task slice after a framework change' as the regression-drill workflow
components.why: The one surveyed benchmark whose subject is the agent FRAMEWORK, not the model

components.tool: OpenSSF Scorecard probe pattern (benchmarks/probes/, one probe_<name>.py + card.yml each)
components.role: Deterministic audit vectors: binary findings JSON (evidence layer) kept separate from weighted/tiered scores (score layer); cards carry motivation/implementation/remediation
components.why: Proven schema keeping months-apart runs comparable even when weights change; tiered scoring matches the determinism maturity ladder (enforced halt > warning > prose)

components.role: Qualitative vectors decomposed into many small binary met/not-met criteria with weights (negative points for violations) and mandatory evidence citation — never Likert holism
components.why: Binary criteria survive judge nondeterminism and diff cleanly version-over-version

components.role: Pin judge model+temperature+rubric version+prompt hash in every result file; re-score the frozen calibration set every run; judge/rubric change = declared metric reset
components.why: Directly answers 'scores comparable across runs months apart'

components.role: Stats brain for k>=5 paired arms only: paired credible intervals and P(A>B) from the per-task score matrix; ICC computed alongside as the determinism/flakiness metric. Never used at n<=3 — those arms report raw counts
components.why: ICML 2025 result: CLT error bars are dishonestly narrow at this bench's n; designed to be vendored as one file

components.tool: miner.py (owned ~150-line transcript miner over ~/.claude/projects JSONL) + ccusage --json cross-check
components.role: Per-phase telemetry: tokens, cost, tool-call counts, Task/agent spawns, wall-clock — attributed to karta phases via session ids recorded by claude_arm.sh and worktree cwd paths; purely post-hoc
components.why: The one quantity NO surveyed tool provides; ccusage's maintained parser is the schema reference when Claude Code upgrades shift the format; native OTel export is the documented fallback (known -p flush caveat)

components.role: Cribbed, not invoked: aggregate_benchmark.py mean/stddev/delta shapes, grader schema with evidence fields, analyzer flake-detection heuristics, blind comparator prompt for vN-vs-vN-1 pairwise judging
components.why: Official Anthropic machinery for the skill-level A/B case; zero install cost

components.role: Grades karta-plan by comparing a synthesized binder against the reference binder of a really-delivered feature (binder-eol-dogfood precedent is the first reference)
components.why: Score the DECISION against a known-good decision, not prose against vibes

components.role: Tier-0 deterministic regression of the six hook guard scripts: recorded stdin JSON payloads (verified top-level agent_type/agent_id shapes), assert exit codes and halt messages
components.why: An afternoon of stdlib pytest beats adopting the 0-star claude-code-hook-tester

components.role: reduce.py folds promptfoo output + miner telemetry + probe findings + calibration scores into the one committed result file; trend.py regenerates benchmarks/results/TREND.md from every committed result.json — the version-over-version report
components.why: Longitudinal git-native comparison is the acknowledged gap in every surveyed tool; this thin owned layer is the bench's whole point

chassis.task_format: One format for both vector families: a task package directory benchmarks/suites/<suite>/tasks/<task-id>/ containing task.yaml + fixture/ (template repo or probe target spec) + verify/ (deterministic verifier script: exit 0/1 + metrics JSON on stdout) + oracle/ (reference solution or reference binder that MUST pass the verifier before the task is admitted) + rubric.yaml (when grading_mode involves a judge). task.yaml fields (terminal-bench-derived, PawBench- and HealthBench-augmented): id; title; canary (anti-leakage GUID present at top of every task file); added_in (karta version — anchors the trend axis); tags; vector: executable|audit; grading_mode: automated|llm_judge|hybrid; instruction (the literal prompt handed to claude -p; for audit tasks, the probe list or rubric target); fixture: {template: ./fixture, reset: fresh-git} for executable tasks or {target: karta-worktree} for audit tasks; arms: named configs (e.g. pack-on: sme:['minimalism','vue'] vs pack-off: sme:[] — version arms are NOT declared here, they come from the runner's CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR pinning); trials: k (default 3, headline tasks 5); timeouts: {agent_sec, verify_sec}; environment: host|docker (docker = the runner stages fixture services into the Incus instance vader-incus-docker-host via tar + incus file push, per the known-good pattern in ~/AGENTS.md — Claude Code itself always runs on the host); verifier: ./verify/check.sh; oracle: ./oracle/; rubric: ./rubric.yaml where rubric.yaml is criteria: [{id, statement, weight, polarity: positive|negative, evidence_required: true}], rubric_version, judge: {model, temperature}. run_bench.py compiles task.yaml files into the suite's promptfooconfig.yaml so authors touch only task packages, never promptfoo config. The existing date-picker fixture (benchmarks/sme/fixtures/date-picker.md) migrates as executable/date-picker with arms pack-on/pack-off, grading_mode hybrid — proving format continuity with the 2026-07-06 precedent.
chassis.stats_discipline: Pairing everywhere, Bayes for intervals only where n supports it, ICC for flakiness, and a declared noise floor. (1) n and k: every executable task runs k>=3 trials per arm (k=5 for headline vectors when the token budget allows); the suite aims for 20-50 tasks sourced from real karta failures, growing incrementally; the README states the current n. (2) Paired arms always: identical fixture tasks across arms and across versions, so every comparison is a question-level paired difference; clustered treatment when fixtures are variants of a shared scenario. (3) Intervals: vendored bayes_evals computes paired credible intervals and P(A>B) at k>=5 — never CLT/SEM error bars at this n; at n<=3 report raw count tables only (per-vector sharpening ruling: intervals at n=3 are theater; raw counts discriminate identically). (4) When a delta is real: reported REAL only when P(A>B) >= 0.90 (or <= 0.10) AND the 90% credible interval excludes zero; single-run deltas under ~3pp (the agentic-noise floor: identical SWE-bench runs vary 2.2-6.0pp even at temp 0) are labeled NOISE regardless; everything between is NO-DETECT at this n, stated as such. (5) ICC committed every Tier-2 cycle, separating 'karta got worse' from 'karta got flakier' — enforced-checks-over-prose changes should RAISE it. (6) Power honesty: the bench README carries a standing statement of the minimum delta detectable at current n*k. (7) Structural counts beat statistics wherever available: spawn counts, retry counts, re-validation passes, git-subprocess counts are expected exactly reproducible — any drift is the finding, no interval needed.
chassis.runner_design: Entry point: python benchmarks/bench/run_bench.py --tier <0|1|2> [--suite S] [--karta-version vX.Y.Z] [--baseline vA.B.C], run on the host (vader), no daemons. (1) PREP — git worktree of the karta repo at the target tag into scratch; build an isolated CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (bench-owned settings + the karta plugin from that worktree installed; real ~/.claude untouched); when --baseline is given, build a second config dir the same way (version arms = two config dirs, same tasks). (2) COMPILE — read every task.yaml in scope, verify each oracle passes its verifier (admission gate), emit the suite's promptfooconfig.yaml with one exec-provider per arm (benchmarks/bench/claude_arm.sh --config-dir <dir> --arm <name>) and the task's assertions. (3) EXECUTE — npx promptfoo@<pin> eval --output scratch/promptfoo-out.json; per trial claude_arm.sh copies the fixture template to scratch, fresh git init, runs claude -p --output-format json under the pinned config dir on the host (subscription auth; no Docker for the default path), runs the verifier, returns output + metadata. Audit suites: provider is python benchmarks/probes/run_probes.py over the karta worktree (no model call) or a judge assertion over karta's own markdown. Docker-needing fixtures (environment: docker) are staged into the Incus Docker host first (tar --exclude=.git -cf, incus file push, incus exec vader-incus-docker-host -- tar -xf && docker compose up -d, --network host so ports reach the host process); Claude Code itself NEVER runs inside Docker. (4) HARVEST — miner.py walks ~/.claude/projects JSONL for the recorded session_ids, cross-checked against npx ccusage session --json. (5) REDUCE — reduce.py merges promptfoo output + telemetry + probe findings + calibration re-scores into benchmarks/results/<date>-v<version>/result.json + summary.md; stats.py (vendored bayes_evals) computes paired intervals, P(A>B), ICC at k>=5. (6) TREND — trend.py regenerates benchmarks/results/TREND.md from all committed result.json files; owner reviews and commits. Bench self-test (run_bench.py --self-test) checks oracle admission, miner schema against a recorded fixture transcript, and reduce.py determinism. The deterministic gate lane has its own thinner entrypoint, benchmarks/gate/run_gate.py (see release-gate-blocking-smoke), which does not require node/promptfoo.
chassis.cadence_tiers: Tier 0 — SMOKE, every version bump (minutes, zero model tokens, BLOCKING pre-tag via release-gate-blocking-smoke): deterministic probes over the karta worktree, pytest golden-payload regression of the six hook guards, oracle-must-pass admission checks, bench self-test. Tier 1 — PULSE, every minor release or notable skill/pack change (~1 hour, modest subscription tokens): headline executable micro-fixtures (date-picker class) at k=3 on the primary A/B arms, the frozen calibration set, and the llm-judged audit vectors over karta's own markdown; results committed. Tier 2 — FULL, monthly or bracketing a major architectural change (several hours, off-hours to respect plan limits): whole suite at k=5, the scoped micro-binder delivery fixture (plan->deliver->verify on a 2-3 item binder — full binder runs strain the one-provider-call model, so the delivery fixture stays deliberately small), full transcript-mined per-phase telemetry, version-vs-baseline arms (two CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIRs), TREND.md regenerated. Ad-hoc — REGRESSION DRILL (PawBench move): before/after a specific change, rerun a fixed task slice with paired stats only.
risks: promptfoo release churn (daily pushes, 0.x): pin in benchmarks/bench/package.json, invoke via npx promptfoo@<pin>; upgrades are deliberate tested events — never floating.

~/.claude/projects JSONL schema is official-but-undocumented and shifts with Claude Code releases: miner.py keeps a recorded fixture transcript as a self-test, cribs from ccusage's maintained parser on breakage; native OTel export is the fallback (collector-during-run cost + known -p flush caveat accepted).

Transcript decay: cleanupPeriodDays is unset (~30d cleanup; parchmark's transcript dir was down to 4 files by 2026-07-17 and the 1.14.0 raws are gone). Set cleanupPeriodDays>=365 in ~/.claude/settings.json, mine within 7 days of each delivery, and treat committed dated JSONs as the durable series — old points are NEVER re-derived from raws.

Subscription-auth headless runs burn plan quota and can hit rate limits at k*tasks*arms scale: tiering caps this; Tier 2 runs off-hours; promptfoo's per-test outputs let reduce.py salvage completed trials mid-run.
Judge drift and rubric edits silently breaking comparability: calibration set + metric-reset rule mitigate, not eliminate — residual risk documented, not papered over.

Whole-binder karta-deliver runs (multi-worktree, tens of minutes) strain promptfoo's one-provider-call-per-test model: keep the delivery fixture to a scoped micro-binder; if the ceiling is hit hard, Inspect AI (+inspect_swe) is the named second chassis for sandboxed long-horizon runs — a migration, not a patch.

Fixture contamination/memorization: absolute scores on public-pattern fixtures are not capability claims; only version-over-version deltas on the same fixture are, since contamination sits on both sides.
Node toolchain in a Python/markdown repo: contained entirely under benchmarks/bench/ (package.json + lockfile); all glue, probes, stats, and reporting stay Python.

Bench rot under a single owner: bench self-test runs in Tier 0; every component is vendored, official, or a ~150-line owned script; bench-self-integrity's mutation drill tests detection power quarterly.
Docker-path fixtures add Incus staging complexity: environment: docker is opt-in per task and expected to stay rare.
Results-directory growth: only reduced result.json + summary.md are committed (raw transcripts stay in scratch, referenced by session_id); older runs compress in place without breaking trend.py.

Headless karta-deliver via claude -p is unproven (benchmarks/sme/README.md 'Automation note'): pilot once before adopting the vectors that depend on it; each such vector carries a manual-capture fallback.

vectors.name: Guard behavior and cross-runtime enforcement matrix
vectors.question: Do karta's guards deny/allow correctly on their matched paths, and which mutation classes escape enforcement entirely — per tool channel inside Claude Code (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit/Bash/Task) and per runtime outside it (Codex CLI, plain terminal git)?
vectors.procedure: Build once: benchmarks/fixtures/hooked-repo/ (git repo with HEAD-committed binder, archived binder, staged-but-uncommitted binder edit, symlink alias to a binder, .karta/sme/ pack, and refs/karta/<slug>/built/<id> with no matching done ref via git update-ref) + benchmarks/flow/mutation-surface.json (versioned manifest of (mutation-class, channel) rows: binder writes, pack writes, refs/karta/* forging, git commit without gate, integration merges, crossed with Write/Edit/NotebookEdit/Bash/Task/codex/terminal; each row enforced|waived:<id> — e.g. a waiver for KARTA_SKIP_GATE, the documented repo-scoped escape hatch; manifest seeded from the hooks dark_areas of the committed audit map benchmarks/flow/seed-map-2026-07-17.json). Family A (every release, deterministic, ~1 min): benchmarks/flow/probe_guards.py runs each hooks/scripts/guard_*.py as a subprocess with cwd=fixture repo — explicitly additive to the scripts' embedded --self-tests, which inject a fake tracked() and never touch real git — feeding crafted stdin payloads per probe id: guarded write (expect exit 2), benign write (expect 0), archived-binder write (expect 2), symlink-alias path, staged-not-committed binder (currently passes — pin as known-gap probe), malformed JSON payload (record fail-open exit 0 for binder/stop guards vs fail-closed exit 2 for auditor-dispatch/writer-confinement, per docs/how-to/hooks.md contract), NotebookEdit-on-pack (pins the live PreToolUse Write-only matcher asymmetry). Output: per-probe-id {expected, actual} table; any changed row vs previous committed run = flagged diff. Family B (every release, static, seconds): parse hooks/hooks.json matchers, cross against the manifest; any row with no matcher and no waiver id = unwaivered BYPASS; additionally fail the run if a guard_*.py exists with no manifest row (anti-staleness check so the hand-written manifest cannot silently rot). Family C (quarterly, live, pruned): run ONLY cells whose enforcement can change — cells with a hooks.json matcher plus any newly-registered runtime surface (e.g. a future Codex hooks manifest). claude -p cells use --output-format stream-json; a cell counts enforced ONLY if the transcript shows a tool call targeting the guarded path that was denied AND git diff --exit-code is clean; clean diff with no attempted call = inconclusive, never enforced; record model id in output. Terminal-git cell (sed -i on a binder + git commit) is scripted with no LLM — a cheap deterministic canary confirming the structural bypass still exists. Structurally-unenforced cells (Codex, plain terminal, Bash-in-CC classes with no matcher) are NOT run live; they live as waivered/bypass rows in Family B. Emit dated JSON committed to benchmarks/flow/results/: per-probe table, unwaivered bypass list (with manifest version), cell matrix with enforced/bypassed/waived/inconclusive/n-a states.
vectors.metric: Headline: unwaivered bypass count (against the named manifest version) + count of cells flipping enforced->bypassed between releases (automatic regression flag) + per-probe expected/actual mismatch count. Informational only (denominators change meaning as manifest/probe set grows): guard payload-matrix pass rate and enforcement coverage ratio.
inputs: benchmarks/flow/mutation-surface.json (new; seeded from benchmarks/flow/seed-map-2026-07-17.json hooks dark_areas)
field evidence: gringotts commit 1f548e5 (committed-binder hand edit from a plain terminal)
vectors.cadence: every-release (Families A+B); quarterly (Family C live cells)
vectors.seed_observation: At least 6 unwaivered bypasses today: Bash writes to binders/packs (cat >, sed -i, git checkout --), git update-ref forging done/accepted refs, Bash git commit with no secret-scan matcher, KARTA_SKIP_GATE=1 self-service, guard_pack_write's NotebookEdit/Edit matcher asymmetry, staged-but-uncommitted binder edits passing the HEAD-only check. Cross-runtime matrix reads ~7/21 applicable cells enforced — CC file-tools 6/6, Bash-in-CC 1/6, Codex 0/6, terminal 0/6 — matching hooks.md's own runtime column and the gringotts 1f548e5 field bypass. Family A also pins which guards fail open (binder immutability, Stop-gate) vs closed (auditor dispatch, writer confinement). Repo-verified during critique: the PreToolUse pack guard matches Write only; guard_binder_immutability checks ls-tree HEAD so staged edits pass.

vectors.name: Prose / schema / guard contradiction count
vectors.question: How many open disagreements exist between what skill prose and published schemas promise and what the guards and validators actually do — documented paths a hook blocks, vocabulary no code reads, schemas that reject shipped artifacts, and fictional safety nets?
vectors.procedure: benchmarks/flow/check_contradictions.py, report-only (always exit 0), run as `uv run --with jsonschema benchmarks/flow/check_contradictions.py --repo . --out benchmarks/results/flow-spec-contradictions/<version>.json`. Canonical tree pinned to skills/ + hooks/ + scripts/ only (mirror copies in .agents/ and plugins/ are scripts/check_shared_copies.py's problem, not this bench's). Four passes. (1) Ref-vocabulary cross-check: extract full-path matches of refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/(\w+) from a FIXED file list (skills/_shared/integration-branch.md, skills/karta-deliver/SKILL.md + references/) and diff two ways against hooks/scripts/*.py string literals and REF_STATES: defined-nowhere-read (today: in-progress, integration-branch.md:52) and written-per-prose-but-absent-from-Stop-gate-REF_STATES (today: accepted vs guard_delivery_stop.py:41). (2) hooks.json pin: validate every event name and matcher tool name against a committed whitelist (benchmarks/flow/claude-code-events.json); unknown names emit status UNKNOWN-EVENT distinct from findings, so a Claude Code upgrade is bench maintenance, not a karta defect; a name within edit-distance 2 of a whitelisted one is a MISSPELLING finding (closes scripts/validate_plugin.py's acceptance of typos like 'PreTooluse'). (3) Promise probes: committed list benchmarks/flow/promises.json; each probe = id + file + sentence-REGEX anchor (never line numbers; anchor miss emits ANCHOR-LOST as its own finding state, never a silent pass) + check. A1: synthesized Edit/Write payloads on a HEAD-committed binder piped to hooks/scripts/guard_binder_immutability.py stdin, assert deny exit (payload fixtures shared with flow-guard-enforcement-matrix's benchmarks/fixtures/hooked-repo/). A2: the between-waves-edit sentence regex in skills/karta-deliver/SKILL.md AND a concrete mechanism anchor named in the probe entry (file + regex that would count as the amend/re-validate mechanism); CONSISTENT only if the sentence is gone OR the mechanism anchor matches. A3 (static; the sandbox replay from the draft is cut — replays belong to dark-midwave-crash-resume): assert that no anchor in skills/karta-deliver/SKILL.md + references matches a committed sanctioned-repair regex set; the missing-repair-path finding (fixed id, seeded from gringotts 1f548e5 / parchmark 76aadba) stays open until a repair mechanism anchor appears or re-plan-to-new-slug is documented as the sanctioned path. (4) Schema-vs-validator duals: vendored fixtures in benchmarks/fixtures/schema-contradictions/ — including a FROZEN copy of gringotts browse-refinements.json (vendor it now from gringotts/.karta/binders/; never reference the live sibling checkout at run time), a minimal shared_terms binder, a backend-only item carrying component_map/icon_map/token_changes, a bogus-extra-key control, and the shipped doc-gardner example config — each validated twice: raw jsonschema against skills/karta-plan/references/binder-schema.json (and doc-gardner-schema.json) vs skills/karta-plan/scripts/validate_binder.py; finding when the two disagree, or when both accept what the karta-plan SKILL.md 'the validator will catch it' promise (sentence-anchored, currently line 281) claims is caught. Findings have content-derived stable ids; resolutions move to a resolved list carrying the resolving commit hash (append-only). Output JSON carries the probe-set id list + a probe-set hash.
vectors.metric: Open contradiction count computed on the INTERSECTION of probe ids with the previous committed run (honest release-over-release, target monotone decrease on that intersection); total open count reported separately as informational; resolved list append-only with resolving commit hashes.
inputs: benchmarks/fixtures/schema-contradictions/ (new; includes a vendored frozen copy of gringotts browse-refinements.json)
vectors.seed_observation: At least 7 open findings today, 6 verified live during critique: the between-waves binder-edit prose vs the immutability hook (field-proven by gringotts 1f548e5, with no sanctioned repair path — which is why parchmark 76aadba burned a full re-plan); the dead in-progress ref contract; the Stop-gate's ignorance of the accepted namespace (REF_STATES at guard_delivery_stop.py:41); validate_plugin accepting arbitrary hooks.json event/matcher spellings; shared_terms binders rejected by the published schema (additionalProperties:false) but accepted via validate_binder's runtime injection at validate_binder.py:133 — every modern gringotts binder is invalid per karta's own schema file; UI fields on non-UI items accepted by both validators despite plan SKILL.md:281's claimed catch; doc-gardner-schema.json executed by no one.

vectors.name: Rules and dark-area ledger burndown
vectors.question: What fraction of karta's flow rules are mechanically enforced (hook or hard-exit script) versus prose-only per subsystem, and how many mapped dark areas are still present, fixed, or mutated at this release?
vectors.procedure: SEED NOW (blocking): the audit map exists only as a session artifact at /tmp/claude-1000/-mnt-agent-storage-vader-src-karta/0fe6f672-22b7-4c17-8abe-f8084f939961/scratchpad/karta-map.json — copy it to benchmarks/flow/seed-map-2026-07-17.json and commit it as provenance before anything else (if the scratchpad has expired, the seed observations embedded in this spec's vectors are the fallback seed source). The map's determinism blocks are prose paragraphs (81 scripted_checks + 107 prose_only_rules ENTRIES, not rules; no per-rule anchors, no class taxonomy), so seeding is an explicit one-time expansion pass: split each entry into per-rule records {id, subsystem, text, class in hook-fail-closed|hook-fail-open|script-hard-exit|script-prose-invoked|prose-only, anchor}. Commit benchmarks/flow/rules-ledger.json + benchmarks/flow/cards/*.yml + benchmarks/flow/run_dark_ledger.py. The ledger is append-only forever after: never bulk re-seed; a future re-audit adds records tagged epoch:2 reported in a separate denominator. ANCHORS: for prose rules, anchor = {file, needle} with needle a distinctive fixed string <=80 chars, matched by fixed-string grep after whitespace normalization (tr -s '[:space:]' ' '), against the canonical skills/ + skills/_shared/ tree ONLY (never .agents/ or plugins/ mirrors — check_shared_copies.py keeps them identical). For enforced rules, anchor = script path; runner asserts existence and runs --self-test (all 6 hooks/scripts/*.py and validate_binder.py/check_shared_terms.py/detect_stack.py support it — verified). Anchor-miss is verdict ANCHOR-STALE (human re-anchors or reclassifies in the same commit as results), NOT auto-MUTATED — SKILL.md prose is rewritten every release and whole-sentence anchors rot. NEW-RULE TRIPWIRE: git diff <prev-release-tag>..HEAD -- skills/ hooks/hooks.json, filter added lines through a regex PINNED IN THE RUNNER, e.g. \b(MUST|must not|never|NEVER|halt|only writer|fail-closed|hard-exit)\b (changing the regex = new epoch for this count); a human classifies each hit into the ledger or a committed not-a-rule allowlist; run FAILS if unclassified_new > 0 or any ANCHOR-STALE unresolved. DARK CARDS: benchmarks/flow/cards/<subsystem>-<nn>.yml {id, subsystem, claim, probe} where probe is a shell one-liner exiting 0=FIXED 1=STILL-DARK 2=ANCHOR-GONE(triage). Seed the ~70 of 79 map dark areas that carry file:line evidence first (verified viable: REF_STATES at guard_delivery_stop.py:41, shared_terms absent from binder-schema.json, in-progress in integration-branch.md all reproduce today); the ~9 judgment-only areas become probe:manual in a separate manual denominator. FIXED cards are never deleted — they gain fixed_in:<version> so burndown is cumulative. OUTPUT: benchmarks/flow/results/<version>-dark-ledger.json with per-class rule counts, enforced ratio per subsystem, (still_dark, fixed_since_last, anchor_gone) triple, unclassified_new; runner diffs against the previous committed results file and prints the delta. HONESTY RULES in the README: the enforced ratio is an index over frozen seed records, not a true rule count; compare each subsystem only to its own history — cross-subsystem ratio comparison is invalid because seed granularity differs per subsystem.
vectors.metric: enforced/(enforced+prose-only) index over frozen seed records, total and per-subsystem (each subsystem compared only to its own history) + (still_dark, fixed_since_last, anchor_gone) triple + cumulative burndown + unclassified-new-rules count (run fails if >0) — compared release over release via committed ledger JSONs.
inputs: benchmarks/flow/seed-map-2026-07-17.json (committed copy of the session audit map; determinism + dark_areas for all 10 subsystems)
previous release's committed ledger runs under benchmarks/flow/results/
vectors.cadence: every-release (seeding expansion is a one-time L-cost pass; per-release run is S-M)
vectors.seed_observation: Baseline: 81/188 entries ~43% enforced overall (an index over map ENTRIES, not individual rules — one entry can bundle ~15 checks); deliver worst at 36% (9/25) with all four core merge invariants (serial FIFO, single tip writer, mandatory re-validation, ref-last ordering) plus wave/frontier computation and Phase-4 anti-forgery entirely prose-only; hooks best at 60%. Hardening velocity ~1 rule/release (2.21.0's Stop-gate moved exactly one rule), making the 107-entry prose backlog a decades-long tail at current pace. Dark side: 0 fixed, ~79 still-dark — spot probes confirm binder-schema.json still lacks shared_terms, guard_delivery_stop.py:41 still reads REF_STATES=(built,done,failed), and integration-branch.md:52 still lists the never-written in-progress ref.

vectors.name: Status and Stop-gate truth on abnormal states
vectors.question: When git state is stranded, forged, or waived, do karta's scripted surfaces (karta_next, serve_status, the delivery Stop-gate) surface the truth with an actionable next step — or render dead ends, silence gates, and badge waived work as PASSED?
vectors.procedure: benchmarks/fixtures/stranded-states/make_state.sh <case-id> fabricates each case in a throwaway repo with plain git (worktree, refs via git update-ref, binder JSON) — S7/S8 are synthesized from scratch inside the script (shapes recorded as git commands), NEVER copied from live parchmark/gringotts at run time. Alongside it, benchmarks/fixtures/stranded-states/expected.json commits one frozen grading anchor per case: {case: {warning_regex, expect_command_nonnull, stop_gate_exit}}. Anchor edits are only legal in the same commit as the surface change they track, and the anchor diff is included in the bench result. Per case run three probes: (1) python3 skills/karta-status/scripts/karta_next.py --json in the fixture repo -> ACTIONABLE iff next_action.command is non-null OR any warnings[] entry matches the case's committed warning_regex — no free-form judgment; (2) echo '<synthetic Stop payload>' | python3 hooks/scripts/guard_delivery_stop.py -> compare exit code (0/2) against stop_gate_exit; (3) serve_status state via a pinned import probe — python3 -c 'import serve_status; print(serve_status._STATE_META[...])' with the exact symbol names recorded in expected.json; if the import fails the case grades FAIL-LOUD (bench errors, never silently skips), and the durable fix is a --snapshot-json mode on skills/karta-status/scripts/serve_status.py added on first breakage. Family S (8 stranded states): S1 built-unmerged mid-wave; S2 all-done-unmerged awaiting human merge; S3 accepted-done item; S4 crashed-build branch with no refs; S5 corrupt/non-dict binder JSON; S6 binder in HEAD but deleted from working tree (gate-vs-engine set-agreement check); S7 post-merge leftovers (parchmark backend-hygiene replica, synthesized); S8 done-item worktree still mounted (gringotts replica, synthesized) — graded per anchors. Family F (4 forgeries via git update-ref): F1 done ref not first-parent-reachable from integration; F2 accepted ref whose target merge lacks Karta-Accept trailers; F3 built ref on no item branch; F4 done ref for an item absent from the binder — DETECTED iff karta_next warns per anchor OR the Stop-gate exits 2; plus one static binary: grep -rEn 'merge-base|--first-parent|rev-list' skills/karta-deliver/ hooks/scripts/ -> scripted-reachability-check-exists yes/no. Family P (fixture-only; the live cross-repo provenance scan from the draft is cut — its sub-metric moves to field-delivery-state-audit): P1 accepted-done fixture must surface a state key distinct from clean done in karta_next --json AND a _STATE_META badge word that is not PASSED; P2 the fabricated accepted-done item must be fully reconstructible (what was waived, why) from refs + merge trailers alone with git for-each-ref + git log --format=%(trailers) — binary. Also assert set(REF_STATES in guard_delivery_stop.py) == set of ref names the engine writes per skills/_shared/integration-branch.md (today this catches the missing 'accepted' entry). Emit per-case stable-ID matrix {S1..S8, F1..F4, P1..P2, gate_engine_ref_agreement} to benchmarks/results/<version>-status-truth.json; new cases get new IDs, existing IDs never change meaning.
vectors.metric: Per-case stable-ID matrix (S1-S8 actionability, F1-F4 forgery detection, P1 accepted-distinct binary, P2 provenance-reconstructible binary, gate_engine_ref_agreement, scripted-reachability-check-exists) — diffed release-over-release by case ID, never by bare fraction.
inputs: benchmarks/flow/seed-map-2026-07-17.json status/deliver dark_areas (committed copy of the audit map)
vectors.cost: M (initial authoring of 12+ fixture states; per-run S thereafter)
vectors.seed_observation: Expect ~2/8 actionable today: S1 reproduces the documented dead-end fallback with empty warnings in the precise state the Stop-gate exists to catch; S5 vanishes from status while crashing the swallowed subprocess; S7/S8 draw no cleanup suggestion — matching what rotted 11 days in parchmark. Forgeries: 0/4 detected — a forged done ref actively SILENCES the Stop-gate's built-without-done check, and no scripted reachability check exists. Provenance: serve_status.py:182 _STATE_META badges accepted-done as PASSED unconditionally, and the real dogfood waves-2-3 ceremony downgrade is machine-unrecoverable today. Verified on disk: karta_next.py --json emits next_action.command + warnings; guard_delivery_stop.py exits 0/2 on stdin payloads; REF_STATES omits 'accepted'.

vectors.name: Mid-wave crash and resume fixture
vectors.question: After a mid-wave worker death (built-unmerged item + zero-commit pending item + stale worktree + existing wave tags), does a resumed karta-deliver reconstruct correctly — merging without rebuilding, not double-tagging, not deleting valid refs?
vectors.procedure: benchmarks/deliver/midwave-crash/ (convention: benchmarks/<name>/ with fixtures/, runner, results/, honesty-rule README). make_fixture.sh builds a throwaway repo with GIT_AUTHOR_DATE/GIT_COMMITTER_DATE and author pinned so all fixture SHAs are byte-stable across quarters. 3-item binder, trivial file-touch items, 'test -f' oracles. TWO variants, both doctrine-reachable (skills/karta-deliver/SKILL.md Step 3 tags wave-N-base only when the merge pass starts — the draft's single fixture with wave-2-base + in-flight builds is an unreachable state): VARIANT A (mid-build crash): wave-1 complete (wave-1-base + wave-1 tags, item-a done ref at its merge commit); wave 2 dispatched — item-b built ref at unmerged item-branch tip, item-c branch with zero commits; NO wave-2-base tag; stale worktree for item-b; orphan stash mimicking the gringotts wave-4 scratch stash. VARIANT B (mid-merge-queue crash): wave-2-base tagged, item-b done (merged), item-c built-unmerged, no wave-2 success tag. make_fixture.sh emits manifest.pre: git for-each-ref refs/karta/ refs/tags/ + branch tips + worktree list + stash list. RUN (n=3 per variant): CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=$(mktemp -d) with the pinned plugin installed; cd fixture; claude -p --dangerously-skip-permissions 'karta-deliver midwave. A prior run crashed mid-wave. At the preflight resume-or-clear prompt choose RESUME — never clear.' (the explicit resume authorization is required: headless -p has no user-input facility and SKILL.md forbids silent resume, so an unadorned prompt dies at preflight and measures nothing). Record claude --version + model ID in the result file. ASSERT via assert_resume.sh diffing manifest.post vs manifest.pre, three tiers: SAFETY invariants, must hold at ANY terminal state including a clean halt — S1 item-a done SHA unchanged; S2 wave-1/wave-1-base tags unmoved, no duplicate or renumbered wave tags; S3 no ref in refs/karta/<slug>/ deleted vs manifest.pre; S4 item-b built tip either still at its ref or an ancestor of a new item-b done merge (merged, never rebuilt); S5 old integration tip is ancestor of new tip (no force-move). PROGRESS, scored only when the run reports success — P1 item-b done exists with built tip as ancestor; P2 item-c done exists and its oracle file present at tip; P3 stale worktree pruned/reused without clobber. OBSERVATIONAL, recorded not scored — O1 wave-N derivation choice (reuse-2 / increment-3 / error-on-duplicate; doctrine is silent, so this is a spec-gap probe, not pass/fail); O2 stash disposition; O3 halt-vs-complete and stated reason. Precondition: pilot the headless -p deliver path once before adopting (benchmarks/sme/README.md notes headless automation is unproven). Kill/resume harness shared with quality-verify-integrity-drills under benchmarks/lib/.
vectors.metric: Headline = safety-invariant pass fraction over S1-S5 (target 1.0; any safety flip across releases is a regression, full stop); progress fraction over P1-P3 reported separately for runs reporting success; observational rows O1-O3 recorded not scored; raw per-assertion count table at n=3 per variant — no interval statistics.
inputs: headless claude -p substrate with isolated CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR
field evidence: gringotts stash@{1} wave-4 crash + live browse-refinements mid-flight state
vectors.seed_observation: Resume will likely complete the pending item (it did in gringotts), but expect failures on hygiene assertions: the stale worktree and stash survive (the gringotts scratch stash was never cleaned), and wave re-tag behavior is undefined — a run that re-derives N over existing tags will either error on the duplicate tag or silently anchor to the stale base, exposing the unspecified-N dark area (O1). The safety/progress split exists because a release that correctly HALTS on the duplicate-tag ambiguity must outscore one that silently anchors wrong.

vectors.name: Consumer-repo delivery state audit
vectors.question: Do completed and in-flight deliveries in consumer repos satisfy the declared git invariants and hygiene doctrine — done refs reachable, wave tags ordered, post-merge cleanup performed, work commits marker-tagged, and committed binders free of manual surgery?
vectors.procedure: Scripts live in benchmarks/flow/ (build once; pure git plumbing + jq, no LLM). GATE: benchmarks/flow/fixtures/delivery-state/ is a committed bare repo built by a deterministic build_fixture.sh (dates pinned via GIT_AUTHOR_DATE/GIT_COMMITTER_DATE) seeding >=1 instance of EVERY reported violation class — forged done ref (not first-parent-reachable via git merge-base --first-parent), wave-tag disorder, wave-base non-ancestor, missing Karta-Accept trailer, leftover refs/**/in-progress, surviving branch/tag/worktree post-archive, complete-but-unarchived binder, UNMARKED work commit, dangling marker id, MANUAL-SURGERY binder blob edit, karta-message stash >48h, missing doctrine ref — PLUS two pinned negative cases that must NOT flag: a legit fast-forward landing and a discarded-integration-branch binder (integration never merged AND binder still live on main). run_all.py must score 100% detection and 0 false positives on the fixture or consumer results are discarded; a violation class with no fixture case may not be reported from the field. Doctrine anchors are content-greps, never line numbers: grep skills/karta-deliver/SKILL.md for 'refs and wave tags remain in git', skills/karta-build/SKILL.md for 'Either form satisfies the requirement', skills/karta-plan/SKILL.md for 'read-only once committed'; hard-fail the run if a sentence is gone (doctrine changed — update linter first). Per repo (parchmark, gringotts, karta itself): (1) lint_delivery_refs.py — per slug under refs/karta/** and .karta/binders/{,archive/}: done-ref first-parent reachability from karta/<slug>/integration or post-merge main; built->done/failed pairing on archived binders; wave-<N>-base ancestor-of wave-<N>, tags strictly ordered; Karta-Accept via git interpret-trailers --parse; no in-progress refs; zero survivors post-archive; complete implies archived (discard-path binders exempt per the fixture ruling). (2) audit_hygiene.py — enumerate refs/tags/branches/worktrees (git worktree list --porcelain)/stashes grouped by slug; classify IN-FLIGHT / STALE (>48h post-delivery measured at the recorded audit_timestamp) / MISSING-DOCTRINE-REF. (3) check_markers.py — git rev-list done ^wave-base --no-merges; subject ^\[karta:item-[a-z0-9-]+\] or Karta-Item trailer; cross-check ids against binder item ids. (4) audit_binder_mutations.py — jq field-level blob diff of post-birth binder commits: RE-PLAN / ARCHIVE-MOVE / METADATA-RETROFIT / MANUAL-SURGERY. ATTRIBUTION (the comparability fix): every finding keys to its delivery cohort — binder slug + delivery date (integration-merge commit or last wave tag) + karta plugin version active at that date from the release ledger. Output committed as benchmarks/flow/results/<date>-<repo>.json recording linter_version and audit_timestamp. TRENDING RULES: version-over-version comparison uses ONLY per-cohort invariant-violation counts and per-binder marker coverage for cohorts delivered since the previous audit; stale_artifact_count and oldest_stale_age_days are point-in-time dashboard numbers, never trends; binder-EOL-never-invoked and cleanup-not-run facts are exported as adoption rows for field-feature-adoption-ledger, not counted as violations here; the provenance-recoverable sub-metric (what fraction of done refs carry any machine-recoverable waiver/ceremony record) also lives here, cut from dark-status-surface-probes. Classes with zero field occurrences ever (failed/accepted/kickback — currently 0/54) report 'n/a (fixture-only)', never '0 violations'. On any linter change, recompute all historical cohorts (git history is immutable, backfill is cheap) so every published trend comes from one linter version.
vectors.metric: Per-cohort (slug + delivery date + plugin version) invariant-violation counts by class + per-binder marker coverage %, trended only for cohorts delivered since the previous audit; stale_artifact_count, oldest_stale_age_days, missing_doctrine_ref_count, provenance-recoverable % as point-in-time dashboard numbers; manual-surgery mutations per delivered binder (target 0); linter detection on the seeded fixture must stay 100% with 0 false positives; never-exercised classes report n/a-fixture-only.
inputs: skills/karta-deliver references (integration-branch.md invariants; SKILL.md refs-survive doctrine, content-grep anchored)
vectors.seed_observation: parchmark: backend-hygiene complete-unarchived with ~19-20 stranded artifacts (10 refs, 4 tags, 5 branches, month-old stash) 11 days post-merge — binder-EOL shipped and never used — and marker coverage 0/5 on backend-hygiene vs 2/3 on deps-upgrade (earlier binders near-100%: a real regression signal the manual dry run caught). gringotts: done-item worktree for wide-screen-layout, 2 stale stashes, upload-reliability landed as fast-forward with no integration merge (pinned negative case), 14/14 markers (both sanctioned forms), and 1 MANUAL-SURGERY (1f548e5 runtime_contract bump). karta itself: 2 MISSING-DOCTRINE-REF slugs (kaizen-frame, codex-119-compatibility). Both repos confirm zero failed/accepted/kickback artifacts across 54 items — those flows have never run in the field; provenance-recoverable reads 0% today.

vectors.name: Feature adoption ledger with mandatory attribution
vectors.question: Which shipped, repo-observable karta features are actually used in each consumer repo — with every absence attributed deterministically — and how fast are features adopted when the repo is active?
vectors.procedure: Maintain benchmarks/field/feature-manifest.json: one entry per observable feature = {id, karta_version, ship_date, detection:{kind: grep|jq|ref, pattern, path}, durable: true|false}. ship_date = committer date of the version-bump commit: git -C /mnt/agent-storage/vader/src/karta log --grep 'chore(release): bump plugin version to <ver>' --format=%cI -1. Ephemeral-state detections (refs/karta/**/failed — karta-deliver deletes the failed ref on accept and rewrites it in revert-the-wave, skills/karta-deliver/SKILL.md ~L85/L130, and git keeps no reflog for refs/karta/**) MUST be durable:false: they report current-state presence only and are EXCLUDED from adoption-lag and from AVAILABLE-BUT-UNUSED. Detect the failure path via durable traces instead: accepted refs (persist post-archive) and waiver/override markers in tracked content. MANIFEST GATE (enforced, not asserted): audit_adoption.py first enumerates releases via git log --grep 'chore(release)' --format='%cI %s' and exits 2 if any release newer than the newest manifest entry lacks both an entry and an explicit {version: V, observable: false} sentinel. ACTIVITY EVENTS pinned to exactly three deterministic commit classes in the consumer repo (committer dates): subject ^plan(karta):, subject ^chore(karta): archive binder, and merge commits of karta/*/integration branches. Nothing else counts. FIRST-USE: tracked-content detections via git log -S<pattern> --format='%H %cI' (earliest hit; stable here because the owner's git doctrine forbids rebase/force-push); durable-ref detections via committer date of the ref target. Once a first_use lands in a committed result JSON it is immutable — later audits carry it forward (append-only ledger), so ref cleanup or marker removal can never flip used->unused. CLASSIFICATION (2 deterministic classes, replacing the undecidable PREDATES/REPO-IDLE split): NOT-EXERCISED-SINCE-SHIP (zero activity events after ship_date; record days_since_last_activity so a human can eyeball idle vs retired) and AVAILABLE-BUT-UNUSED (>=1 activity event after ship_date, detection never fired) — the only alarm class. OUTPUT: commit benchmarks/field/results/<ISO-date>-<repo>.json containing the full per-feature attribution table; headline = AVAILABLE-BUT-UNUSED ids plus count/denominator with denominator always shown; adoption_lag_days per feature; median only when n>=5; negative lags allowed, labeled pre-release-dogfood (real precedent: 1.18.0 was delivered by karta on the real repo before its version bump), excluded from the median.
vectors.metric: AVAILABLE-BUT-UNUSED feature ids with count/denominator always shown, per repo + per-feature adoption_lag_days (median only at n>=5; negative lags labeled pre-release-dogfood, excluded from median); release-over-release comparison = per-feature status-transition diff on stable feature ids, never aggregate-count deltas.
inputs: benchmarks/field/feature-manifest.json (new, grows one entry — or an observable:false sentinel — per release as a release-checklist forcing function)
karta repo release timeline (chore(release) version-bump commits)
consumer repo binder JSONs, refs, configs, git grep surface (parchmark, gringotts)
benchmarks/flow/seed-map-2026-07-17.json features_never_exercised lists (committed copy of the audit map)
vectors.seed_observation: gringotts (active this week on 2.21.0) scores 4 AVAILABLE-BUT-UNUSED — karta-validate (despite design-sourced binders carrying design_facts), debt markers, smoke oracle type, and the failure path (tracked via durable traces: accepted refs + markers; current-state failed-ref presence is durable:false); parchmark attributes most absences as NOT-EXERCISED-SINCE-SHIP (no activity since 07-06) except binder-EOL and debt markers, which were live-and-ignored. Median adoption lag for adopted features is under one day (settings binders 45 min after 1.8.0, kaizen 30 min after 1.13.0) — so persistent non-adoption during active periods is a prune-or-fix signal, not noise.

vectors.name: Companion loop coverage and health (doc-gardner + kaizen)
vectors.question: Do the opt-in companion loops fire on every eligible delivery and produce additive edits that stick — gardner coverage per delivery, kaizen edits surviving without human counter-correction, and neither loop's config going stale?
vectors.procedure: benchmarks/field/audit_companions.py --repo <path> [--head <sha>], stdlib-only python3, one JSON row per repo committed as benchmarks/field/results/YYYY-MM-DD-companions.json. Every row records the analyzed HEAD sha; all history reads walk first-parent of the default branch only (never --all, never live branches — integration branches get pruned). (1) Enable epochs: parse `git log -p --follow --first-parent -- .karta/doc-gardner.json .karta/kaizen.json` for 'enabled' value transitions; active epoch = latest transition to true (--diff-filter=A alone misses disable/re-enable toggles). (2) Delivery denominator: commits adding a file under .karta/binders/archive/; emit slug + committer date as a list, not just a count. (3) Gardner coverage: for each archived slug dated inside a gardner-enabled epoch, match subject '^docs: gardner <slug>$' anywhere in first-parent history; for each whiff, auto-classify timing-race iff the oldest commit on the delivery merge's second-parent lineage predates the enable commit, else true whiff; emit slug + both timestamps. (4) Kaizen additive floor: for each '^kaizen: ' commit, per touched .karta/sme/*.md, extract active/tombstone ids before and after by importing the parser from skills/karta-kaizen/scripts/validate_packs.py (grammar '- [ ] <prefix>.<n> — text', tombstone '- ~~<id>~~ retired: <reason>') — do not re-implement it; FAIL if an active id vanishes without a tombstone, WARN if a surviving id's rule text shrinks >20% chars. (5) Correction incidents: non-kaizen commits touching a kaizen-touched pack file 0-14 days AFTER the kaizen commit (window starts at 0 — gringotts 08bc02a landed 17 minutes after dd80e01 and must count; the draft's 48h floor missed its own field evidence). Right-censor: kaizen commits younger than 14d at run time are flagged window-open and excluded from the closed denominator, so the number is independent of run date. Emit each incident as (kaizen sha, correcting sha, delta-minutes, subject) — an incident list, not a rate, at these Ns. (6) Spawn utility: substantive kaizen commits (seed commit excluded) and delivery count in the kaizen epoch, raw counts. (7) Config staleness: deliveries since the last commit touching each config, plus the current focus string printed beside the last 5 delivery slugs — surface-only, no semantic match scoring, no mtime. All fractions reported as n/N with underlying lists, never bare percentages.
vectors.metric: Gardner coverage as n/N with per-slug list (timing-race whiffs auto-attributed) + kaizen additive-floor violations (target 0) + correction-incident list (kaizen sha, correcting sha, delta-minutes; 0-14d window, right-censored) + kaizen substantive edits per delivery (raw counts) + config staleness in deliveries-since-touch — all n/N with underlying lists, trended per-delivery to feed the kaizen-phase-2 build decision. Honest caveat carried in every row: deliveries carry no plugin-version stamp, so this is loop health over calendar time, not a controlled version A/B.
inputs: 'docs: gardner' and 'kaizen:' commit streams (contractual grammars: skills/karta-doc-gardner/SKILL.md:51, skills/karta-kaizen/SKILL.md:60)
.karta/sme/ pack edit history + the parser in skills/karta-kaizen/scripts/validate_packs.py
field evidence: parchmark #127/#128 timing-race whiffs + stale focus string; gringotts dd80e01 -> 08bc02a 17-minute human rescope
vectors.seed_observation: parchmark: gardner 8/10 with both whiffs auto-attributed to the enable-timing race; config 5 deliveries stale (focus string still says 'aligned with note tag editing behavior' after five backend-only deliveries); kaizen 0 edits over its post-enable deliveries — spawn utility 0, a near-pure tax. gringotts: gardner 3/3 (upload-reliability correctly excluded as pre-enable); kaizen 1 edit, additive-floor clean, corrected by a human in 17 minutes — incident (dd80e01, 08bc02a, 17 min), the first hard datapoint for whether kaizen phase 1 lacks scoping judgment.

vectors.name: SME pack, mirror, and pin static integrity suite
vectors.question: Do all pack surfaces validate and stay in sync — built-ins, the 3 skill mirrors, consumer overlays — and does every binder's pinned pack set equal what the documented matching rule computes from detect_stack, with no dead/overbroad match tokens and no unpropagated upstream fixes?
vectors.procedure: Layout: benchmarks/sme-static/ holds run.sh, match_pins.py, cross_prefix.py, seed_drift.py, consumers.json (enrolled repo paths; each run records the SHA probed), exceptions.json (skip-list with one-line justification per entry), findings/<date>-<version>.json. PROBE 1 pack_integrity: (a) python3 skills/karta-kaizen/scripts/validate_packs.py skills/_shared/sme/*.md with platform-native.md on the committed exceptions.json skip-list — the karta-plan SKILL sme section already declares it 'shared reference data, not a pack'; encoding the skip (vs adding frontmatter) avoids changing shipped prompt text. Target 0 findings after exceptions. (b) uv run scripts/check_shared_copies.py — this already asserts all 3 references/sme mirrors byte-equal _shared; do NOT re-validate mirror files individually (identical bytes, pure noise). (c) validate_packs.py over each consumers.json repo's .karta/sme/*.md; size warnings counted separately from failures. (d) cross_prefix.py (~30 lines, committed): prefix->basename map over _shared + all overlays; fail on a prefix claimed by two basenames or an overlay using a PREFIXES entry registered to a different pack. PROBE 2 match_pins.py — committed ONCE, never re-derived from prose: implements the karta-plan SKILL sme matching steps 1-4 (overlay-over-builtin by basename, disabled suppresses, always:true pins, whole-token case-insensitive equality vs detect_stack dependencies/languages, platform-native skipped); embeds sha256 of the SKILL.md section text and exits 2 'matching rule changed — re-verify implementation, update hash' on mismatch; --self-test fixtures include the templ-vs-github.com/a-h/templ non-match. Per consumer: contract mode = python3 skills/karta-plan/scripts/detect_stack.py <root>; coverage mode = union of detect_stack over every dir <=3 deep holding package.json/pyproject.toml/go.mod/requirements*.txt/Cargo.toml — diagnostic only (labels the documented rule's monorepo blind spot), never the conformance oracle; the coverage-mode diagnostic MUST keep emitting the unmatched-ecosystem list (stacks with no pack — e.g. JVM/Swift/ML manifests detect_stack does not cover), because that list is the surviving carrier of the owner's 'fillable gaps' question. For each binder under .karta/binders/ + archive/ that HAS an sme key, emit {binder, era, pinned, expected_contract, expected_coverage, underivable_pins, missing_pins}; era = plugin version in force at the binder's commit date (karta release tags); headline conformance % computed only over current-era binders, older eras in a frozen backfill table, sme-less pre-feature binders counted excluded not failing. PROBE 3 seed_drift.py — no sync-commit archaeology (no sync marker exists): for each overlay whose basename exists in skills/_shared/sme/, compare overlay content against every historical blob of the built-in (git -C <karta> rev-list HEAD -- skills/_shared/sme/<f>.md; git show <sha>:<path>). Classify IDENTICAL (== HEAD blob), UPSTREAM-UNPROPAGATED (== older blob), LOCAL-ADDITIVE (contains every HEAD-blob line and match-token set is a superset of the built-in's), DIVERGENT (else). Token audit reusing the match_pins matcher: dead token = matches nothing in the union of contract+coverage detect_stack outputs across all consumers.json repos at their recorded SHAs (corpus recorded in findings JSON); overbroad token = equals a detect_stack language literal in a pack whose basename is not that language. OUTPUT: one findings JSON per run — probe-card rows {probe, outcome, file, remediation} plus {plugin_version, consumer_shas, pack_list, binder_era_counts}; committed.

Pack retained if bite_with<=1/5 while fixture live. vectors.name: Context budget and deterministic latency floors
vectors.question: How many bytes/estimated tokens of skill+reference text does each karta phase force into a session (does any always-loaded file exceed the Read cap?), how much of the loaded set is duplicated — and what fixed latency do hook chains, the precommit gate, status derivation, and SessionStart injection impose on ordinary actions?
vectors.procedure: Two probes, one dated JSON committed to benchmarks/perf/results/YYYY-MM-DD.json; the first run bootstraps budgets, later runs diff against ABSOLUTE budgets committed in scenarios.json (never against the previous run — a vs-previous check is a compounding ratchet). (1) benchmarks/perf/context_budget.py + hand-authored benchmarks/perf/scenarios.json — each scenario's (build-lean, build-ui, plan-ui, plan-policy, deliver, verify-full, validate, status) always-loaded set is derived from the SKILL.md texts and pinned in scenarios.json (the audit map's context_cost fields were session prose, not a committed input); the script FAILS if `grep -oE 'references/[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+\.md' plugins/karta/skills/*/SKILL.md` names a file absent from scenarios.json (no silent drift of the loaded set). Per file record bytes, lines, est tokens = bytes/3.8 (trend-only, never an assertion — the estimator and the real Read cap disagree: 78,273-byte karta-build/SKILL.md scores ~20.6k estimated tokens yet truncates in reality). Hard assertions: (a) no always-loaded file > 55,000 BYTES — calibrated to the observed Read-cap truncation of karta-build/SKILL.md at line 383 (= 56,297 bytes); recalibrate the byte cap only if the harness cap changes, with a note in scenarios.json; (b) per-scenario total bytes <= its committed absolute budget; budget edits require a rationale line in the commit. Duplication: fraction AND absolute count of lowercased whitespace-collapsed 8-word shingles appearing in >1 loaded file, per scenario, with the top-5 offending file pairs named. (2) benchmarks/perf/latency_probe.sh: for each matcher entry in hooks/hooks.json (repo root — NOT plugins/karta/hooks/, which does not exist) pipe checked-in synthetic payloads (benchmarks/perf/payloads/: binder-write.json, pack-write.json, confined-write.json, stop.json) into each guard script; 21 reps, discard rep 1, report min and median ms; floor assertions on MIN (min > 300 ms per guard fails; per-Write chain = sum of the 3 PreToolUse guard mins + PostToolUse) — min-of-N because wall-clock medians on this shared host (Incus VMs, browsers) are noisy. Time `uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py` end-to-end (fails > 5 s) and one docs-only commit in a scratch clone with hooks installed (fails > 5 s). karta_next scaling: generate synthetic repos B in {1,10,50} x 5 items (script writes refs/karta/** + binder JSONs); PRIMARY metric = exact git-subprocess count per skills/karta-status/scripts/karta_next.py run, captured by a PATH shim that increments a counter file then execs real git — assert the counts fit count = a + b*B exactly across all three B (integer, deterministic; breaks the moment anyone adds a per-binder or per-item extra call); wall time at each B recorded as secondary with no assertion. Time hooks/scripts/inject_karta_status.py at B=10 (min-of-21, 300 ms floor).
vectors.metric: Per-scenario bytes vs committed absolute budgets + 55,000-byte always-loaded-file cap pass/fail + duplication ratio and absolute count with top-5 offending pairs; per-guard/chain/commit-gate/injection floors on min-of-21 ms against fixed thresholds; karta_next git-subprocess count must fit count = a + b*B exactly at B=1/10/50 (primary), wall time secondary unasserted.
inputs: benchmarks/perf/scenarios.json (new, hand-authored; replaces the audit map's context_cost prose)

Baselines: build-lean ~1,006 lines (~50k est. tokens) before touching project code; deliver's duplication ratio flags the verbatim double 5-gate table and 4x-restated single-writer rule.
